qa_pairs.question: What is the mandatory first step for principal investigators and sponsored users to gain access to any computational resource offered by the Alliance?
qa_pairs.answer: Before using any computational resource, principal investigators (PIs) and any sponsored user needing those resources must first request access to the desired system(s) by visiting https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_systems. Failing to complete this step means users will not be able to log in to the desired system.

qa_pairs.question: How can Principal Investigators request additional project and nearline storage resources on Alliance general purpose clusters without a RAC application, and what are the maximum limits?
qa_pairs.answer: Principal Investigators (PIs) can request additional storage resources by sending an email to support@tech.alliancecan.ca with details of their storage needs. These requests do not require a RAC application. The maximum limits are 40 TB of project storage and 100 TB of nearline storage, which represent the total amount across all clusters such as Fir, Nibi, Rorqual, and Narval where RAS storage is available.

qa_pairs.question: How are CPU resources made available to research groups with an active Alliance account, and what is the typical average utilization for those using their Default RAP?
qa_pairs.answer: CPU resources are available for 'opportunistic use' to all research groups with an active Alliance account. Research groups utilizing their Default RAP may be able to use, on average, up to 200 core years on each cluster. This 200 core years is a variable target and not a reservation or cap, meaning actual utilization can vary based on job characteristics and overall cluster demand.
